  "completed_at_ms": 1780525943721,
  "command": [
    "/usr/bin/zsh",
    "-lc",
    "sed -n '1,220p' services/coding/README.md"
  ],
  "cwd": "/home/cachebag/rafael",
  "parsed_cmd": [
    {
      "type": "read",
      "cmd": "sed -n '1,220p' services/coding/README.md",
      "name": "README.md",
      "path": "services/coding/README.md"
    }
  ],
  "source": "unified_exec_startup",
  "stdout": "# services/coding\n\n`coding` is Rafael's GitHub App worker. It listens for selected GitHub webhook\nevents, decides whether the event should start a coding run, asks the local\nmodel for a plan and bounded code actions, verifies the result, then publishes a\nbranch and pull request through the GitHub App installation.\n\nThis is not a general-purpose bot. It is wired for the local Rafael setup:\n\n- model API: `http://rafael:8080/v1`\n- model name: `Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-14B-Instruct-GGUF:Q4_K_M`\n- GitHub App slug: `netshared`\n- implementation label: `netshared:implement`\n- command mention: `@netshared`\n- default bind address: `127.0.0.1:3030`\n\nThe model endpoint is served by `infra/systemd/llama-server.service`.\n\n## Commands\n\nRun commands from the repository root:\n\n```bash\ncargo run -p coding -- check-config\ncargo run -p coding -- serve\n```\n\n`check-config` loads the environment and logs the resolved high-level settings.\nUse it before starting the webhook server.\n\n`serve` starts the GitHub webhook receiver on `RAFAEL_BIND` and requires\n`RAFAEL_GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. The only route is:\n\n```txt\nPOST /webhooks/github\n```\n\nFor a one-off issue run without a webhook:\n\n```bash\ncargo run -p coding -- issue-triggered \\\n  --repo cachebag/rafael \\\n  --issue 123 \\\n  --trigger manual \\\n  --actor cachebag \\\n  --installation-id \"$RAFAEL_GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID\"\n```\n\nIf `--installation-id` is omitted, `RAFAEL_GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID` must be\nset. `--run-id` is optional; without it the service generates a timestamped run\nid.\n\n## Webhook Triggers\n\nThe server verifies `X-Hub-Signature-256` with\n`RAFAEL_GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. It also requires `X-GitHub-Event` and\n`X-GitHub-Delivery`; the delivery id becomes the run id.\n\nAccepted issue triggers:\n\n- `issues:labeled` when the label matches `RAFAEL_IMPLEMENT_LABEL`\n- `issue_comment:created` from a trusted user with `@netshared implement` or\n  `@netshared retry`\n- `issues:assigned` to `RAFAEL_COLLABORATOR_LOGIN`, only when\n  `RAFAEL_ENABLE_ASSIGNMENT_TRIGGER=true`\n\nAccepted pull request revision triggers:\n\n- `issue_comment:created` on a pull request from a trusted user with\n  `@netshared revise` or `@netshared retry`\n- `pull_request_review:submitted` from a trusted user when the review state is\n  `changes_requested` or `commented`\n- `pull_request_review_comment:created` from a trusted user with\n  `@netshared revise` or `@netshared retry`\n\nThe worker ignores repositories outside `RAFAEL_ALLOWED_REPOS`, events from\nuntrusted users where trust is required, issues with blocking labels, approved\nreviews, closed pull requests, and pull requests whose head branch is in a\ndifferent repository.\n\n## Run Flow\n\nFor issue runs, the worker:\n\n1. Creates a run directory and writes `state.json`.\n2. Creates a GitHub App installation token.\n3. Fetches repository, issue, and comment context.\n4. Creates an isolated checkout under `RAFAEL_WORKDIR`.\n5. Creates a branch named from the issue labels and title, such as\n   `feat/issue-12-add-webhook-receiver`.\n6. Writes `context.json` and asks the model for `plan.md`.\n7. Runs the constrained change loop and records `transcript.jsonl`.\n8. Runs verification, with one bounded repair attempt after failures.\n9. Commits, pushes, and creates or updates a pull request when verification\n   allows publishing.\n\nFor pull request revision runs, the worker checks out the pull request head\nbranch directly, records `pr-state.json` and `review-feedback.json`, applies a\nrevision plan, then pushes back to the existing pull request branch.\n\n## Filesystem Layout\n\nThe local example environment uses:\n\n```txt\nRAFAEL_WORKDIR=/home/cachebag/.local/share/rafael/worktrees\nRAFAEL_RUNS_DIR=/home/cachebag/.local/share/rafael/runs\n```\n\nIssue run artifacts are written under:\n\n```txt\n$RAFAEL_RUNS_DIR/<owner>__<repo>/issue-<number>/<run-id>/\n```\n\nPull request revision artifacts are written under:\n\n```txt\n$RAFAEL_RUNS_DIR/<owner>__<repo>/pr-<number>/<run-id>/\n```\n\nCommon artifacts:\n\n- `state.json`\n- `context.json`\n- `plan.md`\n- `transcript.jsonl`\n- `diff.stat`\n- `verification/`\n\nCheckouts are separate from run artifacts and use the same\n`<owner>__<repo>/issue-<number>/<run-id>/checkout` or\n`<owner>__<repo>/pr-<number>/<run-id>/checkout` shape inside `RAFAEL_WORKDIR`.\n\n## Verification\n\nVerification commands come from repository context plus\n`RAFAEL_VERIFY_COMMANDS`. If neither provides commands, the worker falls back\nbased on detected project type:\n\n- Rust workspace: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, then\n  `cargo test --workspace --all-features`\n- Rust package: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, then `cargo test --all-features`\n- Go: `go test ./...`\n- Node: `npm test`\n- Python: `python -m pytest`\n\nVerification commands are parsed directly, not run through a shell. Shell\ncontrol operators and variable expansion are rejected. Child processes also have\n`RAFAEL_*` variables removed from their environment.\n\nBy default, publishing requires verification to pass. If no verification command\nis selected, the run completes locally but will not publish unless\n`RAFAEL_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_PUBLISH=true`.\n\n## Environment\n\nThe tracked example is `services/coding/.env.example`.\n\nRequired for all modes:\n\n| Variable | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `RAFAEL_GITHUB_APP_ID` | GitHub App ID. |\n| `RAFAEL_GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH` | Path to the GitHub App private key PEM. |\n| `RAFAEL_ALLOWED_REPOS` | Comma-separated allowlist such as `cachebag/rafael`. |\n\nRequired for `serve`:\n\n| Variable | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `RAFAEL_GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | GitHub webhook secret used for HMAC verification. |\n\nRequired for manual CLI runs unless passed as `--installation-id`:\n\n| Variable | Purpose |\n| --- | --- |\n| `RAFAEL_GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID` | GitHub App installation ID. |\n\nModel defaults:\n\n| Variable | Default |\n| --- | --- |\n| `RAFAEL_MODEL_BASE_URL` | `http://rafael:8080/v1` |\n| `RAFAEL_MODEL_NAME` | `Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-14B-Instruct-GGUF:Q4_K_M` |\n\nGitHub behavior defaults:\n\n| Variable | Default |\n| --- | --- |\n| `RAFAEL_GITHUB_APP_SLUG` | `netshared` |\n| `RAFAEL_COLLABORATOR_LOGIN` | `<slug>[bot]` |\n| `RAFAEL_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL` | `https://api.github.com` |\n| `RAFAEL_IMPLEMENT_LABEL` | `netshared:implement` |\n| `RAFAEL_COMMAND_MENTION` | `@<slug>` |\n| `RAFAEL_TRUSTED_USERS` | empty |\n| `RAFAEL_BLOCKING_LABELS` | `no-ai,needs-human,blocked` |\n| `RAFAEL_ENABLE_ASSIGNMENT_TRIGGER` | `false` |\n| `RAFAEL_QUIET_COMMENTS` | `false` |\n| `RAFAEL_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | collaborator login |\n| `RAFAEL_GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | GitHub noreply address built from app id and collaborator login |\n\nWorkspace and limit defaults:\n\n| Variable | Default |\n| --- | --- |\n| `RAFAEL_BIND` | `127.0.0.1:3030` |\n| `RAFAEL_WORKDIR` | `/var/lib/rafael/worktrees` |\n| `RAFAEL_RUNS_DIR` | `../runs` relative to `RAFAEL_WORKDIR` |\n| `RAFAEL_MAX_RUN_MINUTES` | `45` |\n| `RAFAEL_VERIFY_COMMANDS` | empty |\n| `RAFAEL_MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS` | `24` |\n| `RAFAEL_MAX_TOOL_RUNTIME_SECONDS` | `900` |\n| `RAFAEL_MAX_FILE_READ_BYTES` | `131072` |\n| `RAFAEL_MAX_WRITE_BYTES` | `262144` |\n| `RAFAEL_MAX_CHANGED_FILES` | `12` |\n| `RAFAEL_VERIFY_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | `600` |\n| `RAFAEL_VERIFY_TOTAL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | `1200` |\n| `RAFAEL_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_PUBLISH` | `false` |\n",
  "stderr": "",

  "source": "unified_exec_startup",
  "stdout": "# rafael\n\nrafael is both my workstation and the name for this monorepo with all the various services, crates and configuration files that power my homelab.\n\n[tailscale](https://tailscale.com/) is used as a mesh VPN between my devices.\n\n## Layout\n\n```txt\ncrates/          shared Rust crates\nservices/        runnable services and CLIs\ninfra/systemd/   systemd service definitions\n```\n\n## Current State\n\nThe active pieces right now are:\n\n- `services/coding`: the GitHub App coding worker.\n- `services/chat`: the local model chat interface.\n- `infra/systemd/llama-server.service`: the user systemd service for the local\n  `llama.cpp` server.\n- `infra/systemd/rafael-chat.service`: the user systemd service for the chat UI.\n\n## Local Model\n\nThe primary local model is currently served via `llama.cpp`.\n\nModel:\n\n```txt\nQwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-14B-Instruct-GGUF:Q4_K_M\n```\n\nEndpoint:\n\n```txt\nhttp://rafael:8080/v1\n```\n\nThe systemd service definition is located at:\n\n```txt\ninfra/systemd/llama-server.service\n```\n\nMore details:\n\n- [infra/systemd/README.md](infra/systemd/README.md)\n- [services/chat/README.md](services/chat/README.md)\n- [services/coding/README.md](services/coding/README.md)\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n",

## Assistant

Implemented the llama-swap migration in the repo.

Changed:
- Added [llama-swap.yaml](/home/cachebag/rafael/infra/systemd/llama-swap.yaml) with five models: `gemma-everyday`, `qwen3-coder`, `gpt-oss`, `qwen-coder-fim`, `gemma-deep`.
- Added [llama-swap.service](/home/cachebag/rafael/infra/systemd/llama-swap.service), conflicting with legacy `llama-server.service`.
- Updated [rafael-chat.service](/home/cachebag/rafael/infra/systemd/rafael-chat.service) to depend on `llama-swap.service` and default to `gemma-everyday`.
- Extended the local client crate to read `/v1/models`.
- Updated chat backend state/message routing so llama-swap’s model list drives the dropdown and selected model.
- Updated coding worker default model to `qwen3-coder` so it still works against the proxy.
- Updated top-level, systemd, chat, and coding docs.

Gemma repos confirmed from HF model cards:
- `ggml-org/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF:Q4_K_M`
- `ggml-org/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-GGUF:Q4_K_M`

Validation run:
- `cargo check -p chat`
- `cargo test -p client`
- `cargo test -p chat`
- `cargo check -p coding`
- `npm run build`
- YAML parsed successfully with Python YAML

One caveat: `systemd-analyze verify --user` could not fully pass on this host because `~/.local/bin/llama-swap` and `target/release/chat` do not exist here yet. It reported missing binaries, not unit syntax errors.

Sources used: [llama-swap README](https://github.com/mostlygeek/llama-swap), [llama-swap config docs](https://github.com/mostlygeek/llama-swap/blob/main/docs/configuration.md), [Gemma E4B GGUF](https://huggingface.co/ggml-org/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF), [Gemma 26B-A4B GGUF](https://huggingface.co/ggml-org/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-GGUF).

## User

very cool. let's talk a bit about how this all works now

so we've basically done a few things- loaded more models, allow for the lazy laoding ofthem and then also, adjusted the rust crate to support this agnostically. meaning all we have to worry about is supplying the models, any future tools can handle manipulating their output with ease right?

## Assistant

Yes, with a couple precise distinctions.

We did not load the models yet. We defined them in [llama-swap.yaml](/home/cachebag/rafael/infra/systemd/llama-swap.yaml), so `llama-swap` can lazily start the right `llama-server` process when a request names one of those model IDs.

The new flow is:

1. Clients talk to one stable endpoint: `http://rafael:8080/v1`.
2. `llama-swap` exposes `/v1/models` from the YAML config.
3. A request to `/v1/chat/completions` includes `"model": "gemma-everyday"` or `"qwen3-coder"`, etc.
4. `llama-swap` checks whether that model is running.
5. If nothing is running, it starts the matching `llama-server`.
6. If another model is running, the `local-models` group forces swap behavior: unload current, then start requested.
7. After 600 seconds idle, the model unloads.

For Rust code, yes: the local client crate is now more provider/model agnostic. It can:
- call OpenAI-compatible chat completions as before
- stream chat completions
- list models from `/v1/models`

The chat app now uses that model list as the local dropdown source of truth, so future local model additions mostly become config work: add one model block to `llama-swap.yaml`, add it to the group members, restart `llama-swap`, and the chat UI should see it.

The caveats:

- The client crate is agnostic at the “OpenAI-compatible text chat” layer, not every possible model feature.
- Current chat message content is a plain string, so Gemma multimodal image/photo input would need extra request types and UI support.
- Tool calling, structured tool schemas, FIM-specific endpoints, embeddings, reranking, etc. would still need explicit client/backend support if you want those workflows.
- `services/coding` now points at `qwen3-coder` by default, but it does not dynamically list models yet. It can still be changed by `RAFAEL_MODEL_NAME`.

So the clean mental model is: `llama-swap` owns model inventory and lifecycle; Rust clients consume a stable OpenAI-compatible API and can discover available model IDs. Future tools should not need to know about ports, process management, VRAM residency, or unload timing.

## User

okay - should i delete llama servivce now? since llama swap handles everything?

## Assistant

Don’t delete it yet. Disable it.

`llama-swap` now owns the public `:8080` endpoint and starts `llama-server` as child processes per model, so the old `llama-server.service` should not be enabled/running. But keeping the unit file is useful as a rollback path.

On `rafael`, after `llama-swap` is installed:

```bash
systemctl --user disable --now llama-server.service
systemctl --user enable --now llama-swap.service
systemctl --user restart rafael-chat.service
```

Then verify:

```bash
systemctl --user status llama-swap
curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/models
pgrep -af "llama-server|llama-swap"
```

Expected idle state: `llama-swap` is running, and no `llama-server` may be running until the first model request.

I’d keep [llama-server.service](/home/cachebag/rafael/infra/systemd/llama-server.service) in the repo as a legacy fallback. If the new setup is stable for a while, removing the installed user unit symlink is fine, but deleting the tracked file is not necessary.

- document llama-swap install, operations, and rollback flow

## User

ok im onthe workstatio nnow, how do i get this running and get all my services updated and restarted

## Assistant

Run these on `rafael`.

First make sure the repo has the new files:

```bash
cd ~/rafael
test -f infra/systemd/llama-swap.service
test -f infra/systemd/llama-swap.yaml
grep -n "qwen3-coder" infra/systemd/llama-swap.yaml
```

If any of that fails, pull/sync the repo changes first.

Install `llama-swap` to the path the unit expects:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.local/bin ~/src
cd ~/src

git clone https://github.com/mostlygeek/llama-swap.git 2>/dev/null || true
cd llama-swap
git pull
make clean all

install -m 0755 build/llama-swap ~/.local/bin/llama-swap
~/.local/bin/llama-swap --version
```

Build the updated services:

```bash
cd ~/rafael

cd services/chat/web
npm install
npm run build

cd ~/rafael
cargo build --release -p chat
cargo build --release -p coding
```

Install/update the user units:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user

ln -sf ~/rafael/infra/systemd/llama-swap.service ~/.config/systemd/user/llama-swap.service
ln -sf ~/rafael/infra/systemd/rafael-chat.service ~/.config/systemd/user/rafael-chat.service

systemctl --user daemon-reload
```

Switch from direct `llama-server` to `llama-swap`:

```bash
systemctl --user disable --now llama-server.service
systemctl --user enable --now llama-swap.service
systemctl --user restart rafael-chat.service
```

Verify:

```bash
systemctl --user status llama-swap --no-pager
systemctl --user status rafael-chat --no-pager

curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/models | jq .
curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3031/api/state | jq '.providers[].id'
pgrep -af "llama-swap|llama-server"
```

Expected before first chat request: `llama-swap` is running, and there may be no `llama-server` child yet. To test lazy loading:

```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"gemma-everyday","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say hello in one short sentence."}]}'
```

Then watch logs:

```bash
journalctl --user -u llama-swap -f
```

## User

yeah its still going. ill let you know when its done

is that normal? is this becasue this is the first time im actualy using these models and they have to download?

## Assistant

Yes, that is normal.

For `gemma-everyday`, the first request took ~27s because llama-swap had to start `llama-server`, and llama.cpp may have downloaded/initialized the GGUF.

For `qwen3-coder`, first run can take much longer because:

- it is a much larger MoE model
- `llama-server -hf unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF:Q4_K_M` may need to download the GGUF first
- it has to unload Gemma before starting Qwen
- it initializes a 65K context with flash attention and q8 KV cache
- `--n-cpu-moe 24` means CPU/RAM offload setup is involved too

While it’s running, in another shell you can watch:

```bash
journalctl --user -u llama-swap -f
```

Or check download/process state:

```bash
pgrep -af "llama-swap|llama-server"
```

After the model is downloaded once, future cold starts should be mostly load/init time rather than network download time.

## Assistant

Everything is running correctly.

Current state:
- `llama-swap.service` is active on `:8080`.
- Old direct `llama-server.service` is disabled.
- Chat is active on `:3031`.
- Chat sees all five models in the dropdown.
- Lazy load works.
- Swap works: Gemma unloaded, Qwen loaded, only one `llama-server` resident.

One note: chat’s active provider is still `gemma-everyday`, which is fine. If you want to switch it from the API instead of the UI:

```bash
curl -fsS -X PATCH http://127.0.0.1:3031/api/settings \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"activeProviderId":"qwen3-coder"}' | jq '.activeProviderId'
```

Otherwise open:

```txt
http://rafael:3031
```

and select the model from Settings.

## User

the models populate in my UI now.

So wait switching models wot alwasy be that slow wil it?

## Assistant

No. The first switch is usually the slowest.

What you just paid for on `qwen3-coder` was likely:

- first HF download or cache population
- `llama-server` startup
- model load from disk into RAM/VRAM
- MoE CPU-offload initialization
- 65K context/KV setup

Future switches to the same model should skip the network download, so they should be faster. But they will not be instant, because your design intentionally unloads the current model before loading the next one. For big models, switching will still mean “stop current process, start new process, load model weights, initialize context.”

Rough expectation:

- `gemma-everyday`: likely seconds to tens of seconds.
- `qwen-coder-fim`: probably tens of seconds.
- `qwen3-coder`, `gpt-oss`, `gemma-deep`: can still be tens of seconds to over a minute depending on disk cache, quant size, context, and `--n-cpu-moe`.

The fast path is repeated requests to the currently loaded model. Once `qwen3-coder` is resident, more `qwen3-coder` requests should be normal inference speed until the 10 minute idle TTL unloads it.

If switching feels too slow later, the main knobs are:
- lower context for heavy models, especially `qwen3-coder -c 65536`
- tune `--n-cpu-moe`
- keep TTL longer than 10 minutes
- use `gemma-everyday` as the default for casual use
- put very small models in a separate non-exclusive group later, but that violates the current “one model resident” simplicity unless we’re careful.

## User

but once the model is loaded, chatting will be instant or near isntant

## Assistant

Yes, once a model is loaded, normal chat requests to that same model should start quickly.

“Instant” has two parts:

- **Request starts / time-to-first-token:** should be near-instant to a few seconds, depending on prompt length and context already in the conversation.
- **Full answer completion:** depends on how many tokens it generates and model speed.

Your Qwen test after load showed strong runtime numbers:

```txt
prompt: ~87 tok/s
generation: ~61 tok/s
```

So once `qwen3-coder` is resident, short prompts should feel responsive. Long conversations still need prompt processing, so a huge chat history can add delay before the first token.

The big delay is only when:
- switching to a different model
- coming back after the idle TTL unloaded it
- first use after model download/cache miss
- asking with a very long context.

## User (checkpoint)

nice. and when i swith in the UI in chat, it should save my referene vrihgt?

## Assistant

Within the running chat service, yes: changing the model in Settings sends `PATCH /api/settings` and updates `activeProviderId`. But I just noticed a persistence edge case for discovered llama-swap models across a chat service restart, so I’m going to patch that instead of giving you a misleading “yes.”The fix is small: the store should preserve an active model id even if it is not in the saved provider list, because llama-swap models are discovered at runtime from `/v1/models`. I’m patching that and adding a regression test.

    if config.settings.active_provider_id.trim().is_empty() {
        config.settings.active_provider_id = config

Diff: /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/src/store.rs
```
static NEXT_ID: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use crate::types::ProviderKind;

    #[test]
    fn repair_config_preserves_runtime_model_active_provider() {
        let default_provider = StoredProvider {
            id: "local-llama-swap".to_owned(),
            name: "Local llama-swap".to_owned(),
            kind: ProviderKind::OpenAiCompatible,
            base_url: "http://rafael:8080/v1".to_owned(),
            model: "gemma-everyday".to_owned(),
            api_key: None,
            system_prompt: None,
        };
        let config = ChatConfigFile {
            providers: vec![default_provider.clone()],
            settings: ChatSettings {
                active_provider_id: "qwen3-coder".to_owned(),
                theme: ThemeName::Charcoal,
            },
        };

        let (repaired, changed) = repair_config(config, &default_provider);

        assert!(!changed);
        assert_eq!(repaired.settings.active_provider_id, "qwen3-coder");
    }
}

```

## Assistant

Tests are compiling; after that I’ll give you the one command to rebuild/restart chat on the workstation.Yes, and I patched a small restart persistence edge case to make that true for discovered llama-swap models.

Behavior now:
- Switching in the UI saves `activeProviderId`.
- It persists in `~/rafael/data/chat/config.json`.
- After `rafael-chat` restarts, it should keep your selected model as long as llama-swap still lists it.

On the workstation, rebuild and restart chat once more to pick up this fix:

```bash
cd ~/rafael
cargo build --release -p chat
systemctl --user restart rafael-chat.service
```

Then if you switch to `qwen3-coder` in the UI, it should stick.

## User

`Image`
 

yoo holy shit i loaded gemma, asked it who it was, and then loaded qwen and asked it who it was and it remebered he chat context from before

`Image`

## Assistant

Yep. That is the chat backend doing the useful part.

The model process does not remember across swaps. When you switch Gemma to Qwen, Gemma is unloaded and Qwen starts fresh. But the chat app stores the conversation messages in `~/rafael/data/chat/conversations/*.json`, and every new request sends the whole conversation history back to whichever model is currently selected.

So from the model’s point of view, Qwen saw something like:

```text
user: Yoooo
assistant: Hello! ...
user: who are you?
assistant: I am Gemma 4...
user: who are you?
```

Then it answered as Qwen. Same persistent thread, different model answering the next turn.

That’s also why long chats can get slower: every turn includes more prior messages in the prompt. But it gives you exactly this cross-model continuity.

## User

is that something i built into my codebase here?

## Assistant

Yes. That behavior is from your `services/chat` backend.

The key path is:

- Conversations are stored as JSON files under `~/rafael/data/chat/conversations/`.
- On each send, the backend loads the current conversation.
- It appends your new user message.
- Then it sends `conversation.messages` to the selected provider/model.
- The assistant response is saved back onto that same conversation.

The relevant code is in [server.rs](/home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/src/server.rs:291): `stream_message_worker` appends the user message, then calls:

```rust
model::stream_chat(
    &provider,
    &conversation.messages,
    state.config.model_timeout,
    ...
)
```

And [model.rs](/home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/src/model.rs:93) converts every stored message into OpenAI-compatible chat messages.

The model process itself has no memory after a swap. Your app is providing the memory by replaying the stored conversation history to whichever model is currently active.

\"No model selected\" : compactModelName(activeProvider.model);\n  const streamPositionKey = useMemo(() => {\n    const lastMessage = conversation?.messages.at(-1);\n    return `${conversation?.id ?? (\n              <button\n                type=\"button\"\n                className=\"icon-button icon-button-subtle\"\n                aria-label=\"Open sidebar\"\n                title=\"Open sidebar\"\n                onClick={onToggleSidebar}\n              >\n                <PanelLeftOpen aria-hidden=\"true\" size={17} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n              </button>\n            ) : null}\n            <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n              <h2 className=\"truncate text-base font-semibold\">\n                {conversation?.title ?? \"New conversation\"}\n              </h2>\n              <p\n                className=\"mt-0.5 truncate text-xs text-[var(--muted)]\"\n                title={providerConnectionTitle(activeProvider)}\n              >\n                {activeProvider === null ? modelLabel : `${activeProvider.name} · ${modelLabel}`}\n              </p>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n          {busy ? <span className=\"status-pill shrink-0\">working</span> : null}\n        </div>\n      </header>\n\n      <div className=\"relative min-h-0 flex-1\">\n        <div\n          ref={scrollRef}\n          className=\"h-full min-w-0 overflow-x-hidden overflow-y-auto px-3 py-4 sm:px-5 sm:py-7\"\n          onScroll={updateFollowFromScrollPosition}\n          onWheel={interruptFollow}\n          onPointerDown={interruptFollow}\n          onTouchMove={interruptFollow}\n        >\n          {loading === \"loading\" ? (\n            <p className=\"text-sm text-[var(--muted)]\">loading</p>\n          ) : conversation === null || conversation.messages.length === 0 ? (\n            <StartPanel\n              providerName={activeProvider?.name ?? \"No provider configured\"}\n              disabled={busy}\n              onSelectPrompt={useStarterPrompt}\n            />\n          ) : (\n            <MessageThread messages={conversation.messages} busy={busy} />\n          )}\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <footer className=\"composer-shell border-t border-[var(--line)] p-3 sm:p-5\">\n        <div className=\"grid gap-3\">\n          {error !== null ? (\n            <div className=\"rounded-md border border-[var(--danger)] bg-[var(--danger-bg)] px-3 py-2 text-sm text-[var(--danger-text)]\">\n              {error}\n            </div>\n          ) : null}\n          <div className=\"rounded-md border border-[var(--line)] bg-[var(--panel)] p-2 shadow-[var(--shadow-soft)]\">\n            <textarea\n              className=\"composer-input max-h-28 min-h-12 w-full resize-none rounded border-0 bg-transparent px-2 py-2 text-base leading-6 text-[var(--text)] outline-none sm:text-sm\"\n              rows={2}\n              placeholder={`Message ${activeProvider?.name ?? \"rafael\"}...`}\n              value={draft}\n              disabled={busy}\n              onChange={(event) => setDraft(event.target.value)}\n              onKeyDown={(event) => {\n                if (event.key === \"Enter\" && !event.shiftKey) {\n                  event.preventDefault();\n                  void submit();\n                }\n              }}\n            />\n            <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3 border-t border-[var(--line)] px-2 pt-2\">\n              <span className=\"truncate text-xs text-[var(--muted)]\" title={activeProvider?.model}>\n                {activeProvider?.chatSupported === false\n                  ? \"Adapter pending.\"\n                  : modelLabel}\n              </span>\n              <button\n                type=\"button\"\n                className=\"button-primary inline-flex shrink-0 items-center gap-1.5\"\n                disabled={!canSend}\n                onClick={() => void submit()}\n              >\n                <SendHorizontal aria-hidden=\"true\" size={15} strokeWidth={2.2} />\n                Send\n              </button>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </footer>\n    </section>\n  );\n}\n\ninterface StartPanelProps {\n  providerName: string;\n  disabled: boolean;\n  onSelectPrompt: (prompt: string) => void;\n}\n\nfunction StartPanel({ providerName, disabled, onSelectPrompt }: StartPanelProps) {\n  return (\n    <div className=\"flex min-h-full items-center justify-center\">\n      <div className=\"start-panel w-full max-w-3xl rounded-md border border-[var(--line)] bg-[var(--panel)] p-5 shadow-[var(--shadow-soft)]\">\n        <div className=\"flex flex-col gap-1 border-b border-[var(--line)] pb-4\">\n          <p className=\"text-base font-semibold text-[var(--text)]\">Start a thread</p>\n          <p className=\"text-sm text-[var(--muted)]\">{providerName}</p>\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"grid gap-2 pt-4 sm:grid-cols-2\">\n          {starterPrompts.map((prompt) => (\n            <button\n              key={prompt}\n              type=\"button\"\n              className=\"starter-prompt\"\n              disabled={disabled}\n              onClick={() => onSelectPrompt(prompt)}\n            >\n              {prompt}\n            </button>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nconst starterPrompts = [\n  \"Help me think through a homelab task\",\n  \"Draft a concise note\",\n  \"Explain a command or error\",\n  \"Sketch a small implementation plan\"\n];\n\nfunction isAtBottom(element: HTMLElement): boolean {\n  return Math.ceil(element.scrollTop + element.clientHeight) >= element.scrollHeight;\n}\n",
  "status": "completed"
}

## Assistant

The build is still running; I’ll wait for it to finish before giving you the restart step.Patched it. After you send a message, the composer now restores focus when the model finishes responding, so you can keep typing without clicking back into the prompt.

Verified with:

```bash
npm run build
```

To apply it on `rafael` if this change is already in your working tree there:

```bash
cd ~/rafael/services/chat/web
npm run build
systemctl --user restart rafael-chat.service
```

position: absolute;\n  top: 50%;\n  right: 0.75rem;\n  pointer-events: none;\n  color: var(--muted);\n  transform: translateY(-50%);\n}\n\n.control-label {\n  color: var(--muted);\n  font-size: 120ms ease;\n}\n\n.sidebar-settings-button:hover:not(:disabled) {\n  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--text) 6%, transparent);\n  color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--text) 86%, var(--muted));\n}\n\n.theme-mode-button {\n  display: inline-flex;\n  min-height: 2.5rem;\n  align-items: center;\n  justify-content: center;\n  gap: 0.45rem;\n  border: 1px solid var(--line);\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  background: transparent;\n  color: var(--muted);\n  padding: 0 0.7rem;\n  font-size: 0.875rem;\n  font-weight: 600;\n  transition:\n    background 120ms ease,\n    border-color ```

## Assistant

The stream path creates an optimistic assistant message only after the first token arrives, so there is currently no visible “thinking/loading” state inside the thread during model load or prefill. I’ll add a reusable `ActivityIndicator` and render it in the message stream only when the latest assistant message has no content yet.

**Tool Call: Read App.tsx**
Status: Completed

(await createConversation());\n      attemptedConversationId = currentConversation.id;\n      if (conversation === null) {\n        setSelectedConversationId(currentConversation.id);\n      }\n      setConversation(optimisticConversation(currentConversation, content, activeProvider?.id));\n      await streamMessage(\n        currentConversation.id,\n        content,\n        activeProvider?.id,\n        {\n          onConversation: (nextConversation) => {\n            setConversation(nextConversation);\n            setSelectedConversationId(nextConversation.id);\n          },\n          onDelta: (delta) => {\n            setConversation((current) =>\n              appendAssistantDelta(current, currentConversation.id, delta, activeProvider?.id)\n            );\n          }\n        }\n      );\n      await refreshState();\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? (\n            <Sidebar\n              state={state}\n              selectedConversationId={selectedConversationId}\n              activeProvider={activeProvider}\n              busy={busy}\n              collapsed={sidebarCollapsed}\n              theme={activeTheme}\n              onNewConversation={handleNewConversation}\n              onSelectConversation={handleSelectConversation}\n              onDeleteConversation={handleDeleteConversation}\n              onPinConversation={handlePinConversation}\n              onOpenSettings={handleOpenSettings}\n              onThemeChange={handleThemeChange}\n              onCollapse={closeSidebar}\n            />\n          ) : null}\n        </div>\n        <ChatPanel\n          conversation={conversation}\n          activeProvider={activeProvider}\n          busy={busy}\n          error={error}\n          loading={loading}\n          sidebarCollapsed={sidebarCollapsed}\n          onToggleSidebar={toggleSidebar}\n          onSend={handleSend}\n        />\n      </div>\n      {settingsOpen && state !== null ? (\n        <SettingsPanel\n          providers={state.providers}\n          activeProviderId={state.activeProviderId}\n          theme={state.theme}\n          onClose={() => setSettingsOpen(false)}\n          onSaved={handleProvidersChanged}\n          onProviderChange={handleProviderChange}\n          onThemeChange={handleThemeChange}\n        />\n      ) : null}\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction optimisticConversation(\n  conversation: Conversation,\n  content: string,\n  providerId: string | undefined\n): Conversation {\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  return {\n    ...conversation,\n    title: conversation.title === \"New conversation\" ? titleFromContent(content) : conversation.title,\n    updatedAt: now,\n    messages: [\n      ...conversation.messages,\n      {\n        id: `pending-user-${Date.now()}`,\n        role: \"user\",\n        content,\n        createdAt: now,\n        providerId\n      }\n    ]\n  };\n}\n\nfunction appendAssistantDelta(\n  conversation: Conversation | null,\n  conversationId: string,\n  delta: string,\n  providerId: string | undefined\n): Conversation | null {\n  if (conversation === null || conversation.id !== conversationId) {\n    return conversation;\n  }\n\n  const messages = [...conversation.messages];\n  const lastMessage = messages.at(-1);\n  if (lastMessage?.id === STREAMING_MESSAGE_ID) {\n    messages[messages.length - 1] = {\n      ...lastMessage,\n      content: lastMessage.content + delta\n    };\n  } else {\n    messages.push(streamingAssistantMessage(delta, providerId));\n  }\n\n  return {\n    ...conversation,\n    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n    messages\n  };\n}\n\nfunction streamingAssistantMessage(\n  content: string,\n  providerId: string | undefined\n): ChatMessageRecord {\n  return {\n    id: STREAMING_MESSAGE_ID,\n    role: \"assistant\",\n    content,\n    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n    providerId\n  };\n}\n\nfunction titleFromContent(content: string): string {\n  const trimmed = content.trim();\n  return trimmed.length > 64 ? titleFromContent(content) : conversation.title,\n    updatedAt: now,\n    messages: [\n      ...conversation.messages,\n      {\n        id: `pending-user-${Date.now()}`,\n        role: \"user\",\n        content,\n        createdAt: now,\n        providerId\n      }\n    ]\n  };\n}\n\nfunction appendAssistantDelta(\n  conversation: Conversation | null,\n  conversationId: string,\n  delta: string,\n  providerId: string | undefined\n): Conversation | null {\n  if (conversation === null || conversation.id !== conversationId) {\n    return conversation;\n  }\n\n  const messages = [...conversation.messages];\n  const lastMessage = messages.at(-1);\n  if (lastMessage?.id === STREAMING_MESSAGE_ID) {\n    messages[messages.length - 1] = {\n      ...lastMessage,\n      content: lastMessage.content + delta\n    };\n  } else {\n    messages.push(streamingAssistantMessage(delta, providerId));\n  }\n\n  return {\n    ...conversation,\n    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n    messages\n  };\n}\n\nfunction streamingAssistantMessage(\n  content: string,\n  providerId: string | undefined\n): ChatMessageRecord {\n  return {\n    id: STREAMING_MESSAGE_ID,\n    role: \"assistant\",\n    content,\n    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n    providerId\n  };\n}\n\nfunction titleFromContent(content: string): string {\n  const trimmed = content.trim();\n  return trimmed.length > 64 ? (await createConversation());\n      attemptedConversationId = currentConversation.id;\n      if (conversation === null) {\n        setSelectedConversationId(currentConversation.id);\n      }\n      setConversation(optimisticConversation(currentConversation, content, activeProvider?.id));\n      await streamMessage(\n        currentConversation.id,\n        content,\n        activeProvider?.id,\n        {\n          onConversation: (nextConversation) => {\n            setConversation(nextConversation);\n            setSelectedConversationId(nextConversation.id);\n          },\n          onDelta: (delta) => {\n            setConversation((current) =>\n              appendAssistantDelta(current, currentConversation.id, delta, activeProvider?.id)\n            );\n          }\n        }\n      );\n      await refreshState();\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to send message\");\n      if (attemptedConversationId !== null) {\n        const updatedConversation = await getConversation(attemptedConversationId).catch(() => null);\n        if (updatedConversation !== null) {\n          setConversation(updatedConversation);\n          setSelectedConversationId(updatedConversation.id);\n          await refreshState();\n        }\n      }\n    } finally {\n      setBusy(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  function handleSelectConversation(id: string): void {\n    setSelectedConversationId(id);\n    if (isMobile) {\n      closeSidebar();\n    }\n  }\n\n  function handleOpenSettings(): void {\n    setSettingsOpen(true);\n    if (isMobile) {\n      closeSidebar();\n    }\n  }\n\n  function openSidebar(): void {\n    if (sidebarOpenFrameRef.current !== null) {\n      window.cancelAnimationFrame(sidebarOpenFrameRef.current);\n    }\n\n    setSidebarMounted(true);\n    sidebarOpenFrameRef.current = window.requestAnimationFrame(() => {\n      setSidebarCollapsed(false);\n      sidebarOpenFrameRef.current = null;\n    });\n  }\n\n  function closeSidebar(): void {\n    if (sidebarOpenFrameRef.current !== null) {\n      window.cancelAnimationFrame(sidebarOpenFrameRef.current);\n      sidebarOpenFrameRef.current = null;\n    }\n\n    setSidebarCollapsed(true);\n  }\n\n  function toggleSidebar(): void {\n    if (sidebarCollapsed) {\n      openSidebar();\n    } else {\n      closeSidebar();\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function handleProviderChange(providerId: string): Promise<void> {\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      const nextState = await updateSettings({ activeProviderId: providerId });\n      setState(nextState);\n      applyTheme(nextState.theme);\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to update provider\");\n      throw cause;\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function handleThemeChange(theme: ThemeName): Promise<void> {\n    setError(null);\n    applyTheme(theme);\n    try {\n      const nextState = await updateSettings({ theme });\n      setState(nextState);\n      applyTheme(nextState.theme);\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to update theme\");\n      throw cause;\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function handleProvidersChanged(provider?: PublicProvider): Promise<void> {\n    const nextState = await refreshState();\n    if (provider !== undefined && provider.chatSupported) {\n      const updatedState = await updateSettings({ activeProviderId: provider.id });\n      setState(updatedState);\n      applyTheme(updatedState.theme);\n      return;\n    }\n    setState(nextState);\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <main className=\"app-shell min-h-dvh text-[var(--text)]\">\n      <div\n        className={[\n          \"chat-layout\",\n          sidebarCollapsed ? ## Assistant

I’m going to render the indicator in the thread when a request is active and the newest visible message is still the user prompt. That makes it cover model loading, prefill, web-search later, and any other “no tokens yet” phase.

**Tool Call: Edit /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/web/src/components/ActivityIndicator.tsx, /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/web/src/styles.css, /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/web/src/components/MessageThread.tsx**
Status: Completed

Diff: /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/web/src/components/ActivityIndicator.tsx
```
interface ActivityIndicatorProps {
  label?: string;
  detail?: string;
  compact?: boolean;
}

export function ActivityIndicator({
  label = "Working",
  detail,
  compact = false
}: ActivityIndicatorProps) {
  return (
    <div
      className={[
        "activity-indicator",
        compact ? "activity-indicator-compact" : ""
      ].join(" ")}
      role="status"
      aria-live="polite"
      aria-label={detail === undefined ? label : `${label}: ${detail}`}
    >
      <span className="activity-bars" aria-hidden="true">
        <span />
        <span />
        <span />
      </span>
      <span className="activity-copy">
        <span className="activity-label">{label}</span>
        {detail !== undefined ? <span className="activity-detail">{detail}</span> : null}
      </span>
    </div>
  );
}

```


Diff: /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/web/src/styles.css
```

.activity-indicator {
  display: inline-flex;
  min-width: 0;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.7rem;
  color: var(--muted);
}

.activity-indicator-compact {
  gap: 0.45rem;
}

.activity-bars {
  display: inline-flex;
  width: 1.15rem;
  height: 1rem;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  gap: 0.16rem;
  color: var(--accent);
}

.activity-bars span {
  width: 0.18rem;
  height: 0.42rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: currentColor;
  opacity: 0.36;
  transform-origin: 50% 100%;
  animation: activity-pulse 960ms ease-in-out infinite;
}

.activity-bars span:nth-child(2) {
  animation-delay: 120ms;
}

.activity-bars span:nth-child(3) {
  animation-delay: 240ms;
}

.activity-copy {
  display: inline-flex;
  min-width: 0;
  align-items: baseline;
  gap: 0.45rem;

Diff: /home/cachebag/rafael/services/chat/web/src/styles.css
```
.activity-indicator-compact {
  transform: scale(0.82);
  transform-origin: left center;
}

.message-bubble-activity {
  max-width: min(7rem, calc(100% - 2rem));
}

.activity-glyph {
  position: relative;
  display: block;
  width: 3rem;
  height: 1.35rem;
  opacity: 0.88;
}

.activity-glyph::before {
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0.12rem 0.22rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background:
    radial-gradient(circle at 20% 62%, currentColor 0 0.13rem, transparent 0.14rem),
    radial-gradient(circle at 52% 24%, currentColor 0 0.12rem, transparent 0.13rem),
    radial-gradient(circle at 82% 58%, currentColor 0 0.11rem, transparent 0.12rem);
  content: "";
  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 0.55rem color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 34%, transparent));
  opacity: 0.42;
}

.activity-thread {
  position: absolute;
  height: 1px;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: linear-gradient(90deg, transparent, currentColor, transparent);
  opacity: 0.28;
  transform-origin: left center;
}

.activity-thread-primary {
  top: 0.78rem;
  left: 0.55rem;
  width: 1.22rem;
  transform: rotate(-31deg);
  animation: activity-thread-glow 1.9s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.activity-thread-secondary {
  top: 0.48rem;
  left: 1.42rem;
  width: 1.18rem;
  transform: rotate(24deg);
  animation: activity-thread-glow 1.9s ease-in-out 420ms infinite;
}

.activity-node {
  position: absolute;
  width: 0.36rem;
  height: 0.36rem;
  border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 62%, transparent);
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--assistant-bg) 76%, currentColor 24%);
  box-shadow:
    0 0 0 1px color-mix(in srgb, var(--assistant-bg) 70%, transparent),
    0 0 0.65rem color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 22%, transparent);
  animation: activity-node-breathe 1.9s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.activity-node-a {
  top: 0.72rem;
  left: 0.42rem;
}

.activity-node-b {
  top: 0.18rem;
  left: 1.42rem;
  animation-delay: 260ms;
}

.activity-node-c {
  top: 0.64rem;
  left: 2.46rem;
  animation-delay: 520ms;
}

.activity-glyph > span:last-child {
  position: absolute;
  top: 0.58rem;
  left: 0.5rem;
  width: 0.28rem;
  height: 0.28rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: currentColor;
  opacity: 0.35;
  offset-path: path("M 0 7 C 8 -6 22 -6 32 5");
  animation: activity-spark 1.9s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes activity-node-breathe {
  0%,
  100% {
    opacity: 0.42;
    transform: scale(0.86);
  }

  48% {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: scale(1);
  }
}

@keyframes activity-thread-glow {
  0%,
  100% {
    opacity: 0.16;
  }

  46% {
    opacity: 0.46;
  }
}

@keyframes activity-spark {
  0% {
    offset-distance: 0%;
    opacity: 0;
    transform: scale(0.7);
  }

  18%,
  72% {
    opacity: 0.48;
  }

  100% {
    offset-distance: 100%;
    opacity: 0;
    transform: scale(1);
  }

```

  "stdout": "import { useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\nimport type { ReactNode, SelectHTMLAttributes } from \"react\";\nimport { ChevronDown, Moon, Sun, X } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport { saveProvider } from \"../api\";\nimport { compactModelName } from \"../display\";\nimport {\n  composeTheme,\n  themeBase,\n  themeMode,\n  themes,\n  toggledTheme,\n  type ThemeBaseName\n} from \"../themes\";\nimport type {\n  ProviderKind,\n  PublicProvider,\n  SaveProviderRequest,\n  ThemeName\n} from \"../types\";\n\ninterface SettingsPanelProps {\n  providers: PublicProvider[];\n  activeProviderId: string;\n  theme: ThemeName;\n  onClose: () => void;\n  onSaved: (provider?: PublicProvider) => Promise<void>;\n  onProviderChange: (id: string) => Promise<void>;\n  onThemeChange: (theme: ThemeName) => Promise<void>;\n}\n\ninterface ProviderFormState {\n  id?: string;\n  name: string;\n  kind: ProviderKind;\n  baseUrl: string;\n  model: string;\n  apiKey: string;\n  systemPrompt: string;\n}\n\nconst emptyProvider: ProviderFormState = {\n  name: \"\",\n  kind: \"open_ai_compatible\",\n  baseUrl: \"\",\n  model: \"\",\n  apiKey: \"\",\n  systemPrompt: \"\"\n};\n\nexport function SettingsPanel({\n  providers,\n  activeProviderId,\n  theme,\n  onClose,\n  onSaved,\n  onProviderChange,\n  onThemeChange\n}: SettingsPanelProps) {\n  const activeProvider =\n    providers.find((provider) => provider.id === activeProviderId) ?? providers[0];\n  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string>(activeProvider?.id ?? \"new\");\n  const [form, setForm] = useState<ProviderFormState>(() =>\n    activeProvider === undefined ? emptyProvider : providerToForm(activeProvider)\n  );\n  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n\n  const selectedProvider = useMemo(\n    () => providers.find((provider) => provider.id === editingId),\n    [editingId, providers]\n  );\n  const mode = themeMode(theme);\n  const switchToMode = mode === \"dark\" ? \"light\" : \"dark\";\n\n  function chooseProvider(id: string): void {\n    setEditingId(id);\n    const provider = providers.find((item) => item.id === id);\n    setForm(provider === undefined ? emptyProvider : providerToForm(provider));\n    setError(null);\n  }\n\n  async function submit(): Promise<void> {\n    setSaving(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      const request = formToRequest(form, selectedProvider);\n      const provider = await saveProvider(request);\n      await onSaved(provider);\n      setEditingId(provider.id);\n      setForm(providerToForm(provider));\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to save provider\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function updateActiveProvider(providerId: string): Promise<void> {\n    setSaving(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      await onProviderChange(providerId);\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to update model\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function updateTheme(themeName: ThemeName): Promise<void> {\n    setSaving(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      await onThemeChange(themeName);\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to update theme\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"settings-overlay\">\n      <section className=\"settings-modal\" role=\"dialog\" aria-modal=\"true\" aria-labelledby=\"settings-title\">\n        <header className=\"settings-header\">\n          <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n            <h2 id=\"settings-title\" className=\"text-base font-semibold\">\n              Settings\n            </h2>\n            {activeProvider !== undefined ? (\n              <p className=\"mt-1 truncate text-xs text-[var(--muted)]\" title={activeProvider.model}>\n                {activeProvider.name} · {compactModelName(activeProvider.model)}\n              </p>\n            ) : null}\n          </div>\n          <button\n            type=\"button\"\n            className=\"icon-button icon-button-subtle\"\n            aria-label=\"Close settings\"\n            title=\"Close settings\"\n            onClick={onClose}\n          >\n            <X aria-hidden=\"true\" size={17} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n          </button>\n        </header>\n\n        <div className=\"settings-body\">\n          <section className=\"settings-section\">\n            <h3 className=\"settings-section-title\">Chat</h3>\n            <div className=\"settings-grid settings-grid-two\">\n              <Field label=\"Active model\">\n                <SelectControl\n                  value={activeProviderId}\n                  disabled={saving || providers.length === 0}\n                  onChange={(event) => void updateActiveProvider(event.target.value)}\n                >\n                  {providers.length === 0 ? <option value=\"\">No providers</option> : null}\n                  {providers.map((provider) => (\n                    <option key={provider.id} value={provider.id} disabled={!provider.chatSupported}>\n                      {provider.name}\n                    </option>\n                  ))}\n                </SelectControl>\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"Theme\">\n                <div className=\"grid grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_auto] gap-2\">\n                  <SelectControl\n                    value={themeBase(theme)}\n                    disabled={saving}\n                    onChange={(event) =>\n                      void updateTheme(\n                        composeTheme(event.target.value as ThemeBaseName, mode)\n                      )\n                    }\n                  >\n                    {themes.map((themeOption) => (\n                      <option key={themeOption.value} value={themeOption.value}>\n                        {themeOption.label}\n                      </option>\n                    ))}\n                  </SelectControl>\n                  <button\n                    type=\"button\"\n                    className=\"theme-mode-button\"\n                    disabled={saving}\n                    title={`Switch to ${switchToMode} mode`}\n                    onClick={() => void updateTheme(toggledTheme(theme))}\n                  >\n                    {switchToMode === \"light\" ? (\n                      <Sun aria-hidden=\"true\" size={15} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n                    ) : (\n                      <Moon aria-hidden=\"true\" size={15} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n                    )}\n                    {switchToMode === \"light\" ? \"Light\" : \"Dark\"}\n                  </button>\n                </div>\n              </Field>\n            </div>\n          </section>\n\n          <section className=\"settings-section\">\n            <div className=\"settings-section-heading\">\n              <h3 className=\"settings-section-title\">Provider</h3>\n              <SelectControl\n                id=\"provider-edit\"\n                className=\"settings-provider-select\"\n                value={editingId}\n                onChange={(event) => chooseProvider(event.target.value)}\n              >\n                {providers.map((provider) => (\n                  <option key={provider.id} value={provider.id}>\n                    {provider.name}\n                  </option>\n                ))}\n                <option value=\"new\">New provider</option>\n              </SelectControl>\n            </div>\n\n            <div className=\"settings-grid settings-grid-two\">\n              <Field label=\"Name\">\n                <input\n                  className=\"control\"\n                  value={form.name}\n                  onChange={(event) => setForm({ ...form, name: event.target.value })}\n                />\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"Type\">\n                <SelectControl\n                  value={form.kind}\n                  onChange={(event) =>\n                    setForm({ ...form, kind: event.target.value as ProviderKind })\n                  }\n                >\n                  <option value=\"open_ai_compatible\">OpenAI compatible</option>\n                  <option value=\"anthropic\">Anthropic</option>\n                </SelectControl>\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"Base URL\">\n                <input\n                  className=\"control\"\n                  value={form.baseUrl}\n                  onChange={(event) => setForm({ ...form, baseUrl: event.target.value })}\n                />\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"Model\">\n                <input\n                  className=\"control\"\n                  value={form.model}\n                  onChange={(event) => setForm({ ...form, model: event.target.value })}\n                />\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"API key\">\n                <input\n                  className=\"control\"\n                  type=\"password\"\n                  value={form.apiKey}\n                  placeholder={selectedProvider?.hasApiKey ? \"stored\" : \"\"}\n                  onChange={(event) => setForm({ ...form, apiKey: event.target.value })}\n                />\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"System\" className=\"settings-field-wide\">\n                <textarea\n                  className=\"control min-h-24 resize-y\"\n                  value={form.systemPrompt}\n                  onChange={(event) => setForm({ ...form, systemPrompt: event.target.value })}\n                />\n              </Field>\n            </div>\n          </section>\n\n          {error !== null ? (\n            <div className=\"rounded-md border border-[var(--danger)] bg-[var(--danger-bg)] px-3 py-2 text-sm text-[var(--danger-text)]\">\n              {error}\n            </div>\n          ) : null}\n        </div>\n\n        <footer className=\"settings-footer\">\n          <button\n            type=\"button\"\n            className=\"button-primary\"\n            disabled={saving}\n            onClick={() => void submit()}\n          >\n            Save provider\n          </button>\n        </footer>\n      </section>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction SelectControl({\n  children,\n  className,\n  ...props\n}: SelectHTMLAttributes<HTMLSelectElement>) {\n  return (\n    <span className={[\"select-shell\", className ?? \"\"].join(\" \")}>\n      <select className=\"control select-control\" {...props}>\n        {children}\n      </select>\n      <ChevronDown\n        aria-hidden=\"true\"\n        className=\"select-chevron\"\n        size={16}\n        strokeWidth={2.1}\n      />\n    </span>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction Field({\n  label,\n  children,\n  className\n}: {\n  label: string;\n  children: ReactNode;\n  className?: string;\n}) {\n  return (\n    <label className={[\"grid gap-2\", className ?? \"\"].join(\" \")}>\n      <span className=\"control-label\">{label}</span>\n      {children}\n    </label>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction providerToForm(provider: PublicProvider): ProviderFormState {\n  return {\n    id: provider.id,\n    name: provider.name,\n    kind: provider.kind,\n    baseUrl: provider.baseUrl,\n    model: provider.model,\n    apiKey: \"\",\n    systemPrompt: provider.systemPrompt ?? \"\"\n  };\n}\n\nfunction formToRequest(\n  form: ProviderFormState,\n  selectedProvider: PublicProvider | undefined\n): SaveProviderRequest {\n  const apiKey = form.apiKey.trim();\n  return {\n    id: selectedProvider === undefined ? undefined : form.id,\n    name: form.name,\n    kind: form.kind,\n    baseUrl: form.baseUrl,\n    model: form.model,\n    apiKey: apiKey === \"\" ? undefined : apiKey,\n    systemPrompt: form.systemPrompt.trim() === \"\" ? undefined : form.systemPrompt\n  };\n}\n",

  "formatted_output": "import { useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\nimport type { ReactNode, SelectHTMLAttributes } from \"react\";\nimport { ChevronDown, Moon, Sun, X } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport { saveProvider } from \"../api\";\nimport { compactModelName } from \"../display\";\nimport {\n  composeTheme,\n  themeBase,\n  themeMode,\n  themes,\n  toggledTheme,\n  type ThemeBaseName\n} from \"../themes\";\nimport type {\n  ProviderKind,\n  PublicProvider,\n  SaveProviderRequest,\n  ThemeName\n} from \"../types\";\n\ninterface SettingsPanelProps {\n  providers: PublicProvider[];\n  activeProviderId: string;\n  theme: ThemeName;\n  onClose: () => void;\n  onSaved: (provider?: PublicProvider) => Promise<void>;\n  onProviderChange: (id: string) => Promise<void>;\n  onThemeChange: (theme: ThemeName) => Promise<void>;\n}\n\ninterface ProviderFormState {\n  id?: string;\n  name: string;\n  kind: ProviderKind;\n  baseUrl: string;\n  model: string;\n  apiKey: string;\n  systemPrompt: string;\n}\n\nconst emptyProvider: ProviderFormState = {\n  name: \"\",\n  kind: \"open_ai_compatible\",\n  baseUrl: \"\",\n  model: \"\",\n  apiKey: \"\",\n  systemPrompt: \"\"\n};\n\nexport function SettingsPanel({\n  providers,\n  activeProviderId,\n  theme,\n  onClose,\n  onSaved,\n  onProviderChange,\n  onThemeChange\n}: SettingsPanelProps) {\n  const activeProvider =\n    providers.find((provider) => provider.id === activeProviderId) ?? providers[0];\n  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string>(activeProvider?.id ?? \"new\");\n  const [form, setForm] = useState<ProviderFormState>(() =>\n    activeProvider === undefined ? emptyProvider : providerToForm(activeProvider)\n  );\n  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n\n  const selectedProvider = useMemo(\n    () => providers.find((provider) => provider.id === editingId),\n    [editingId, providers]\n  );\n  const mode = themeMode(theme);\n  const switchToMode = mode === \"dark\" ? \"light\" : \"dark\";\n\n  function chooseProvider(id: string): void {\n    setEditingId(id);\n    const provider = providers.find((item) => item.id === id);\n    setForm(provider === undefined ? emptyProvider : providerToForm(provider));\n    setError(null);\n  }\n\n  async function submit(): Promise<void> {\n    setSaving(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      const request = formToRequest(form, selectedProvider);\n      const provider = await saveProvider(request);\n      await onSaved(provider);\n      setEditingId(provider.id);\n      setForm(providerToForm(provider));\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to save provider\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function updateActiveProvider(providerId: string): Promise<void> {\n    setSaving(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      await onProviderChange(providerId);\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to update model\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function updateTheme(themeName: ThemeName): Promise<void> {\n    setSaving(true);\n    setError(null);\n    try {\n      await onThemeChange(themeName);\n    } catch (cause) {\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"failed to update theme\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"settings-overlay\">\n      <section className=\"settings-modal\" role=\"dialog\" aria-modal=\"true\" aria-labelledby=\"settings-title\">\n        <header className=\"settings-header\">\n          <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n            <h2 id=\"settings-title\" className=\"text-base font-semibold\">\n              Settings\n            </h2>\n            {activeProvider !== undefined ? (\n              <p className=\"mt-1 truncate text-xs text-[var(--muted)]\" title={activeProvider.model}>\n                {activeProvider.name} · {compactModelName(activeProvider.model)}\n              </p>\n            ) : null}\n          </div>\n          <button\n            type=\"button\"\n            className=\"icon-button icon-button-subtle\"\n            aria-label=\"Close settings\"\n            title=\"Close settings\"\n            onClick={onClose}\n          >\n            <X aria-hidden=\"true\" size={17} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n          </button>\n        </header>\n\n        <div className=\"settings-body\">\n          <section className=\"settings-section\">\n            <h3 className=\"settings-section-title\">Chat</h3>\n            <div className=\"settings-grid settings-grid-two\">\n              <Field label=\"Active model\">\n                <SelectControl\n                  value={activeProviderId}\n                  disabled={saving || providers.length === 0}\n                  onChange={(event) => void updateActiveProvider(event.target.value)}\n                >\n                  {providers.length === 0 ? <option value=\"\">No providers</option> : null}\n                  {providers.map((provider) => (\n                    <option key={provider.id} value={provider.id} disabled={!provider.chatSupported}>\n                      {provider.name}\n                    </option>\n                  ))}\n                </SelectControl>\n              </Field>\n              <Field label=\"Theme\">\n                <div className=\"grid grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_auto] gap-2\">\n                  <SelectControl\n                    value={themeBase(theme)}\n                    disabled={saving}\n                    onChange={(event) =>\n                      void updateTheme(\n                        composeTheme(event.target.value as ThemeBaseName, mode)\n                      )\n                    }\n                  >\n                    {themes.map((themeOption) => (\n                      <option key={themeOption.value} value={themeOption.value}>\n                        {themeOption.label}\n                      </option>\n                    ))}\n                  </SelectControl>\n                  <button\n                    type=\"button\"\n                    className=\"theme-mode-button\"\n                    disabled={saving}\n                    title={`Switch to ${switchToMode} mode`}\n                    onClick={() => void updateTheme(toggledTheme(theme))}\n                  >\n                    {switchToMode === \"light\" ? (\n                      <Sun aria-hidden=\"true\" size={15} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n                    ) : (\n                      <Moon aria-hidden=\"true\" size={15} strokeWidth={2.1} />\n                    )}\n                    {switchToMode === \"light\" ? \"Light\" : \"Dark\"}\n                  </button>\n                </div>\n              </Field>\n            </div>\n chooseProvider(event.target.value)}\n              >\n                {providers.map((provider) => (\n                  <option key={provider.id} value={provider.id}>\n className=\"settings-grid settings-grid-two\">\n              <Field label=\"Name\">\n                <input\n                  className=\"control\"\n                  value={form.name}\n function Detail({ label, value }: { label: string; value: string }) {
  return (
    <div className="settings-detail">
      <span className="control-label">{label}</span>
      <span className="settings-detail-value" title={value}>
        {value}
      </span>
    </div>
  );
}

function providerKindLabel(provider: PublicProvider): string {
  if (provider.kind === "open_ai_compatible") {
    return "OpenAI compatible";
  }
  return "Anthropic";
}

```

## User

Very very nice

question0 i am trying to wrap my head around the architecture

so does it work like this...

do i have crates/, which controls the core functionaltiy of my agents tooling and functioanlity things lke tool registries, memory, etc. and then do i have services which arethe literal services i decide to build?

For example, is this how chat web works??

web interface -> rust backend using abrasctions defined in crates/ in order to support thigns like model switching, output streaming, etc?

## Assistant

Yes, that’s the right mental model with one refinement: `crates/` does not “control” the app. It provides shared building blocks. `services/` compose those blocks into actual runnable products.

In this repo:

`crates/`
Shared Rust libraries:
- `client`: OpenAI-compatible model client, streaming, `/models` listing.
- `common`: shared helpers like safe IDs, slugging, redacted secrets.
- `config`: env/config helpers.
- `memory`, `tool-registry`, `tools/*`: reusable agent/tooling pieces for services that need them.

`services/`
Actual apps/daemons:
- `services/chat`: chat web app + Rust backend.
- `services/coding`: GitHub App coding worker.
- others are service-specific binaries.

For chat specifically, the flow is:

```text
React web UI
  -> /api/* on Rust chat backend
  -> services/chat state/store/model logic
  -> crates/client OpenAI-compatible client
  -> llama-swap at http://rafael:8080/v1
  -> one lazy-loaded llama-server child
  -> selected local model
```

Model switching works like:

```text
UI Settings "Active model"
  -> PATCH /api/settings activeProviderId
  -> backend saves selected model id
  -> next chat request uses that model id
  -> llama-swap loads/swaps the matching model
```

Streaming works like:

```text
llama-server streams tokens
  -> crates/client reads OpenAI SSE stream
  -> services/chat converts it to browser SSE events
  -> React app appends deltas into the visible assistant message
```

Conversation memory works in `services/chat`, not inside the model:

```text
stored conversation JSON
  -> backend sends previous messages with each request
  -> whichever model is active sees the same chat history
```

So future architecture can stay clean:

- Put reusable primitives in `crates/`.
- Build actual user-facing workflows in `services/`.
- Keep llama-swap responsible for local model lifecycle.
- Let services talk to models through `crates/client`, without caring which process/model is resident.

## User

very nice. i like how this is shaping out to be.

one thing that i htink migh be worth looking into...right nowi have everyhing in systemd files and i manaully start them up but maybe its worth creatign a docker setup early on ebfore i start extendign my homelabs fucntioanltiy>

## Assistant

Yes, worth thinking about now, but I would not rush to Dockerize everything.

For your current setup, I’d use a **hybrid** approach:

```text
Host systemd:
  llama-swap
  llama-server children
  GPU/CUDA/model runtime

Docker / Compose later:
  web services
  APIs
  databases
  queues
  search/indexing
  dashboards
  background workers
```

The reason: your model runtime is very host-specific. It depends on RTX 4080 access, CUDA paths, `~/src/llama.cpp`, HF cache, VRAM/RAM behavior, and systemd lifecycle. Dockerizing that too early adds NVIDIA container toolkit, GPU device passthrough, model cache volumes, CUDA compatibility, and networking complexity.

Your current systemd setup is actually good for the GPU layer.

Where Docker becomes valuable is when you add things like:

- Postgres / SQLite backup sidecars
- Redis / queue workers
- web search service
- embedding/index service
- memory service
- dashboard/admin UI
- reverse proxy
- background automation services

Then Compose gives you clean dependency wiring:

```text
chat -> model endpoint
chat -> memory db
coding -> model endpoint
search-tool -> browser/search container
```

But `llama-swap` can stay as the stable host model endpoint:

```text
containers talk to http://host.docker.internal:8080/v1
or host network / host-gateway
```

The early thing I’d do is not “move everything to Docker.” I’d make the repo **container-ready**:

- clear env vars per service
- `.env.example` files
- stable data dirs
- health endpoints
- `just` commands for build/deploy/restart
- maybe `infra/docker/compose.yml` for non-GPU services later
- keep systemd units for host-level services

So my recommendation:

1. Keep `llama-swap` + GPU model runtime on systemd.
2. Keep current services on systemd for now since they’re simple and CI deploys them.
3. When you add a database, queue, search, memory, or browser tool, introduce Docker Compose for those supporting services.
4. Later, if the app surface grows, containerize `services/chat` and `services/coding`, but still point them at host `llama-swap`.

That gives you the best of both: stable bare-metal GPU performance, plus Docker where dependency management actually helps.

## User

oka ywe will ignore docker for now then...

## Assistant

That’s the right call for now.

The current shape is clean enough:

```text
systemd -> stable host services
llama-swap -> model lifecycle
Rust services -> app logic
crates -> shared primitives
CI runner -> build/deploy
```

Docker will make sense when you introduce a real supporting dependency like Postgres, Redis, search/indexing, or a browser automation service. Until then it would mostly add deployment surface area without solving a current problem.

registry = ToolRegistry::new();\n        registry\n            .register(\n                ToolDefinition::new(\n                    \"search\",\n                    \"search text\",\n                    serde_json::json!({\"type\": \"object\"}),\n                )\n                .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(\n            registry\n                .validate_invocation(&ToolInvocation::new(\"search\", serde_json::json!({})).unwrap())\n                .is_ok()\n        );\n        assert!(\n            registry\n                .validate_invocation(&ToolInvocation::new(\"write\", serde_json::json!({})).unwrap())\n                .is_err()\n        );\n    }\n}\n",
  "status": "completed"
}
